title: Prompt-Shield Signature (v1)
description: Schema for a single threat-intel signature entry consumed by the prompt-shield firewall. One signature = one YAML file under v1/source/sig-NNNN.yml; CI merges them into the published v1/signatures.json feed.

id.description: Stable identifier. Must match exactly ^sig-[0-9]{4}$ (e.g. sig-0001 .. sig-9999). IDs are immutable once published.

type.description: Matcher engine. 'regex' = Python re-compatible regex; 'substring' = case-insensitive literal substring; 'semantic_hash' = 64-bit SimHash fingerprint of the canonical attack text.

pattern.description: The matcher payload itself. For regex it must compile under Python's re module. For substring it is a literal needle. For semantic_hash it is a 16-hex-character lower-case fingerprint.

category.description: Coarse-grained taxonomy bucket. Constrained to a closed enum so dashboards, dedup, and per-category telemetry stay consistent across clients.

severity.description: Suggested action tier. critical = block + alert; high = block; medium = flag/score; low = log only.

attack_class.description: Threat-model axis. Orthogonal to 'category': category is what the attacker is trying to do, attack_class is how they deliver it.

origin.description: Provenance bucket for attribution and trust-weighting.

reference.description: Specific citation: URL, probe id, paper section, or community PR/issue number. Required so every signature is auditable.

first_seen.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the attack was first observed or published. Immutable.

description.description: One-line human summary. Hard-capped at 200 chars so it renders cleanly in dashboards and audit logs.

confidence.description: Curator's confidence the pattern is a true positive in the wild. Clients may multiply this into their own scoring.

tags.description: Free-form labels (lowercase, kebab/snake-case, 2-32 chars). Use for facets the closed enums don't cover.

last_updated.description: ISO-8601 date the entry was last edited. Optional on first publish; CI may auto-stamp.

notes.description: Long-form curator notes — caveats, false-positive risks, suggested rollout guidance.

language.description: ISO 639-1 two-letter code. Set ONLY for non-English variants (e.g. 'fr', 'ja'). Absence implies English / language-agnostic.
